page.title: Electricity
page.description: Energy is one of the largest costs and environmental impacts in the accommodation sector.

validation.invalidOperatingDays: The year has a maximum of 365 days.
validation.missingFields: Please complete all fields in the tables: Operational Data and ENERGY (kWh).
validation.missingConsent: You must agree to data processing.

consent.label: By submitting, I agree to the processing of the provided data.

modal.title: Download PDF
modal.description: Your data has been submitted. You can now download the PDF report.

1.title: Guesthouse / Pension - only accommodation, no gastronomy
1.tooltip: Typically smaller facilities focused on lodging without dining services; lower energy use from kitchens.

2.title: Hotel / Pension - only continental breakfast, reception, basic services
2.tooltip: Basic services with limited food preparation; moderate energy use from breakfast and reception operations.

3.title: Self-catering accommodation, small accommodation
3.tooltip: Guests cook on-site; energy use depends on room occupancy and appliance efficiency.

4.title: Hotel with restaurant - full gastronomy
4.tooltip: Full restaurant operations increase energy use (kitchen, refrigeration, ventilation).

5.title: Hotel with wellness / pool / conferences - high consumption
5.tooltip: Wellness, pools, and conferences significantly raise heating, water, and HVAC demand.

electricity_grid.name: Electricity – grid
electricity_grid.explanation: Electricity purchased from the national grid (without renewable certificates).

electricity_renewable.name: Electricity – renewable
electricity_renewable.explanation: Electricity supplied with Guarantees of Origin or Renewable Energy Certificates – may be reported as zero-emission (market-based).

natural_gas.name: Natural gas
natural_gas.explanation: Fuel gas used for heating or water heating.

heating_oil.name: Heating oil
heating_oil.explanation: Diesel or light fuel oil used in boilers.

lpg.name: LPG
lpg.explanation: Liquefied petroleum gas used for heating or cooking.

biomass.name: Biomass (biogenic)
biomass.explanation: Wood pellets, chips, or similar – only biogenic fraction counted.

district_heating.name: District heating
district_heating.explanation: Purchased heat from a district system; factor depends on local mix.

other.name: Other
other.explanation: Any other energy source not listed above.

summary.title: What we measure?
summary.whyText: Energy intensity directly affects operating costs and carbon footprint. Tracking consumption helps reveal savings quickly.
summary.quote: "Energy efficiency is the fastest, cheapest and cleanest way to reduce emissions." — International Energy Agency

energyConsumption.totalEnergyAlt: Total energy consumption per m² per year

report.stable: Stable trend – maintain current practices and keep staff motivated.
report.slightImprovement: Slight improvement observed – monitor if the trend continues.
report.slightIncrease: Slight increase in consumption – analyze possible reasons.
report.significantImprovement: Great! Significant improvement – include in ESG report and inspire others.
report.significantIncrease: Significant increase – detailed analysis and action plan recommended.
report.majorImprovement: Major improvement – analyze what worked and standardize the approach.
report.majorDeterioration: Major deterioration – immediate action and optimization needed.

energyPerM2.high: Conduct an energy audit and implement efficiency improvements.

energyPerRoomNight.slightly: Optimize heating, lighting, and equipment operation.
energyPerRoomNight.high: Review room-level energy consumption and improve controls.

emissionsPerM2.slightly: Consider partial transition to renewable sources.
emissionsPerM2.high: Reduce fossil fuel use and upgrade to low-carbon technologies.

emissionsPerRoomNight.slightly: Consider renewable electricity or carbon offsetting.
emissionsPerRoomNight.high: Develop a carbon-reduction plan and improve energy efficiency.
